one_time_description: Gain on investment in South Beach Group of Partnerships (modified equity method)
economic_notes: First Nation holds 1/7 ownership interest in South Beach Group of Partnerships including South Beach Casino Limited Partnership and related entities. Also holds 12.5% beneficial ownership in Shawano Wapunong Investments Corp. which invests in commercial real estate in Winnipeg.
notes: Fiscal year is 2022, not 2024. Audit report dated May 3, 2023 — well after required filing deadline, causing non-compliance with First Nations Financial Transparency Act. Auditor's report includes Emphasis of Matter regarding a demand loan payable up to $4,669,000 used to pursue a legal claim against the Government of Canada; no settlement asset recorded. First Nation is non-compliant with CMHC replacement reserve requirements (underfunded by $109,055) and CMHC operating surplus reserve (no separate account established). Non-compliant with Financial Administration Law. Two subsidiaries consolidated: Black River Development Corporation and Black River Land Development Ltd. South Beach Group of Partnerships accounted for by modified equity method (GBE). expenses_housing combines Housing ($507,305) and Subsidized Housing ($839,285). expenses_salaries_total taken from cash flow statement cash paid to employees. total_government_transfers = ISC $15,329,439 + CMHC $365,206 + Manitoba Justice $58,000 + Assembly of Manitoba Chiefs $257,502 = $16,010,147. osr = $17,119,805 - $16,010,147 = $1,109,658 (includes rent, tobacco tax, other revenue, deferred revenue movements). annual_debt_service from cash flow repayment of long-term debt $460,941.
warnings: One-time items ($1) represent 0.0% of revenue — surplus may not reflect ongoing operations
High ISC dependency (89.54%) — operations heavily reliant on federal transfers
